reasoning: ERC20 approve(spender, amount) call targeting the consolidation address attributed to the June 2023 Atomic Wallet incident. The selector-only match (no argsTemplate gating) is intentional: any approval to this counterparty enables the drain regardless of token or amount. MALICIOUS at high confidence, severity high: a successful approval here lets Lazarus-attributed infrastructure pull the entire approved balance asynchronously.

Token approval call where the spender is the Pink Drainer affiliate contract. Drainer kits depend on victims signing arbitrary approve calls on phishing pages; once granted, the kit sweeps balance on a separate transaction. Selector-only match keeps the antibody robust to token rotation. Verdict MALICIOUS: there is no benign reason for an agent to approve this counterparty.

ERC721 and ERC1155 setApprovalForAll(operator, true) where the operator is an Inferno Drainer collector. This selector grants whole-collection withdraw rights to the operator and is the dominant primitive for NFT phishing drains in Q4 2024 incident reports. MALICIOUS verdict reflects the irreversibility within the approval window: once the kit's sweeper sees the approval, it pulls the entire collection in a single transaction.
Demo fixture: approve to phishing-kit drainer. Synthesized for the demo.
Demo fixture: setApprovalForAll to drainer. Synthesized for the demo.
Demo fixture: transferFrom called on honeypot ERC20 - sells revert post-buy.

EIP-2612 permit() call submitting a victim's gasless signature with the Atomic Wallet drainer as the spender. Permit-based drains are particularly damaging because the victim's wallet UI shows a signature request rather than a transaction, lowering the suspicion threshold. MALICIOUS at the highest confidence: the only reason to submit a permit naming a sanctioned-attribution drainer is to execute a drain. Severity reflects the full-balance approval semantics of permit.
